sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: QYLG ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:QYLG

feed.title: Parsons gets 2 more option years on classified U.S. contract
feed.summary: Parsons (NYSE: PSN) announced a classified contract award worth over $47 million for continued work with the U.S. government. This extension builds on a task the company has performed for more than six years and includes two additional one-year option periods. The classified nature of the work limits public disclosure but provides near-term revenue visibility, complementing other recent contract wins.

feed.title: Wisconsin regulators approve two new solar projects for $360 million
feed.summary: Wisconsin utility regulators have approved We Energies' purchase of two new solar projects, Good Oak and Gristmill, in Columbia County. These projects, costing $360 million, will generate 165 megawatts and could primarily power large data centers, including those for Microsoft and Vantage. This initiative aligns with WEC Energy Group's goal of achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2050.

feed.title: Mirova Decreases Stake in Eli Lilly and Company $LLY
feed.summary: Mirova has significantly reduced its stake in Eli Lilly and Company by 23.3% in the third quarter, selling 6,722 shares, though LLY remains its 10th-largest holding. Despite this decrease, Wall Street analysts largely maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus on the stock, projecting a target price of approximately $1,221.44. The drug manufacturer shows strong potential with promising Phase 3 retatrutide results and substantial manufacturing investments, yet faces challenges from pricing pressures and increased competition, notably from Novo Nordisk's new higher-dose Wegovy.

feed.title: Darden leaders share strategy behind LongHorn Steakhouse's 7.2% sales surge
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants executives are attributing LongHorn Steakhouse's 7.2% same-store sales growth to a long-term strategy focused on quality and culture. This success comes despite increasing operating costs and pressures in the casual dining industry. LongHorn Steakhouse currently operates over 500 locations.

feed.title: US auto safety regulator closes defect petition on over 2 million Tesla vehicles
feed.summary: The U.S. auto safety regulator, NHTSA, has closed a defect petition covering approximately 2.26 million Tesla vehicles. The regulator found no evidence of a safety-related flaw concerning alleged unintended acceleration due to pedal misapplication. NHTSA stated that very few relevant incidents occurred and data showed vehicles responded as intended.

feed.title: HPQ.SW HP Inc. (SIX): CHF15.25 intraday on volume spike 20 Mar 2026: watch CHF14.70 support
feed.summary: HP Inc. (HPQ.SW) experienced a significant intraday volume spike on March 20, 2026, with its stock price dropping to CHF15.25. This move, characterized by a volume 33.33 times its average, indicates strong selling interest despite the company showing value-oriented metrics like a low PE ratio and strong free cash flow yield. Meyka AI rates HPQ.SW as a "HOLD" with a projected yearly target of CHF12.80, suggesting potential downside, and advises traders to monitor the CHF14.70 support level and upcoming June 3, 2026 earnings.

feed.title: MGM Resorts International Aktie: Digital Expansion and Casino Resilience Drive Investor Interest Ami
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International (MGM) is gaining investor interest due to strong Q4 results and strategic digital expansion, particularly through its MGM Digital segment including LeoVegas. The company balances traditional casino strength, driven by Las Vegas Strip operations and a recovering tourism sector, with a growing online gaming presence. This hybrid model offers diversification and sustainable growth opportunities for investors, especially as global tourism rebounds and online betting regulations evolve.

feed.title: Ford Motor Company is investigating accident that killed employee
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company is investigating an industrial accident at its Sharonville Transmission plant that resulted in the death of employee Gregory Knopf. The incident occurred when a press machine undergoing routine maintenance was accidentally turned on, pinning Knopf. This article also covers other unrelated labor disputes, including potential strikes by Teamsters at INEOS and nurses at Corewell Health East, and a call from the AFL-CIO regarding voter suppression legislation.
